results.rendered_html: mahar ind. (for mahas) the fourth of the seven worlds which rise one above the other (supposed to be the abode of those saints who survive a destruction of the world,  Pur. ;  Vedāntas. ; cf.  IW. 55 n. 2 ).

results.rendered_html: mahar und maharloka m. N. der vierten von den sieben aufsteigenden Welten  BHĀG. P. 2,1,28 . 8,20,33 .  MĀRK. P. 101,25 .  VEDĀNTAS. (Allah.) No. 70 .  ĀRUṆIKOP. _in_ Ind. St. 2,178 .  SIDDHĀNTAŚIR. 3,43 .  VP. 213 . 632 .  BHĀG. P. 2,5,38 .  MĀRK. P. 46,39 .  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 69,b,12 .  PAÑCAR. 2,2,58 . Entstanden aus mahas Grösse, welches  TAITT. UP. 1,5,1 . 3. fgg.  als 4te Vyā- [Page5-0615] -hṛti erscheint.

results.rendered_html:
mahar(mahas) ind.
1 The fourth of the seven worlds which rise one above the other from the earth (being between svar and janas); (maharloka also in this sense).
2 A kind of vyāhṛti q. v.
